transcript:
Luke Judges: Hey everyone, good to see you. Glad to share the stage with the favorite mid-tier manager.

Vibhu Norby: So I'm Vibhu, I'm the representative of the Riddler alt of Bearable Guy 123 589, you know all the memes. Also work at the Solana Foundation in product marketing. Please introduce yourself.

Luke Judges: Sure, yeah. So, hi everyone, I'm Luke Judges. I lead the Partner Success team for RippleX. RippleX is a specific business unit inside Ripple Labs, the wider organization, that's supportive of the XRP Ledger, the open-source public blockchain, and XRP, the native digital asset of that chain. So, pleasure to share the stage with you, man.

Vibhu Norby: Yeah man, it's really amazing to have you here. So, we kind of honestly kind of broke the internet in the last like week. I think our some of the announcements were like number one on all of Twitter basically for engagement. What did we announce? Like talk about it.

Luke Judges: Sure, yeah, so I mean one, congratulations, like that in terms of engagement is insane and I think it's a testament to the XRP community, the Solana community, two of major crypto ecosystems. I think today I want to clarify that kind of Ripple Labs and RippleX isn't announcing anything ourselves, but we're here to support two prominent ecosystem members. So Hex Trust and LayerZero have announced that they're issuing XRP and wrapped XRP onto the Solana blockchain. So I'm super excited about the opportunities that brings up and and yeah, pleasure to announce.

Vibhu Norby: Yeah, I think we kind of talked about this but I think we have a lot more in common than most people kind of think. I think we have really deep storylines and and lore and history in different ways. And so like the more I got to know about the XRP community and Ripple itself as well, the more excited I got about bringing trying to figure out a way to bring the asset to Solana where it can be used in DeFi and lending markets and earn yield and all that kind of stuff. You talk about like what was exciting about it from Ripple's perspective?

Luke Judges: For sure. So I think there's like there's three things that I think make the Solana ecosystem an exciting place to launch XRP. New users, new demand, and market depth. So in terms of users, I think you've got a number of tier one wallets such as Phantom that have say 20 million active wallets that can now access and use XRP. New demand, you've got a number of financial primitives and actually kind of more bespoke applications such as prediction markets, perps DEXs, that we can now get XRP kind of deeply embedded in. And I think in terms of market depth, there's a number of kind of Solana native tokens that we can pair XRP with which I think will be very complementary to XRP the digital asset. Like I the way I've been thinking about this is like if you think of digital assets as a similar way to currencies, the US dollar would not be the global reserve currency if you could only buy it in America and the United States. And so I think it's very interesting if we distribute XRP into other blockchains and you think of them as other countries. I think that's a very interesting value proposition for us and I think Solana is the perfect ecosystem to start.

Vibhu Norby: And while we have you here, like is there anything about Solana specifically? I know you have some history in the ecosystem, but was there is there a reason why maybe Solana was near the top of the list for working on this interoperability?

Luke Judges: Yeah, I think it's those two things in terms of active wallet account. I think look, there's there's millions of XRP holders across the globe. They are vocal. And I think those that that kind of demographic of people matched with the extreme number of Solana developers that are interested in building utility, I think that can be a pretty formidable force if both of those things are combined. So yeah, super excited to see what what will come out. And actually I really encourage the Solana developers here today to build utility for XRP on Solana. I think that's a pretty interesting dynamic and it's something that traditional finance is not is not ever going to do, right? Like that interoperability doesn't work.

Vibhu Norby: So this isn't it for what you guys are doing here. Can you talk about what's coming?

Luke Judges: Yeah, sure. So like look, the the XRP Ledger as a technology stack has been an island for about a decade. We are now heavily heavily leaning in to interoperability. So I think there's like a number of different ways where XRP will move across a variety of crypto ecosystems via interoperability partners. You've seen probably publicly that we've partnered with Wormhole, Axelar, LayerZero, all kind of interoperating with the XRP Ledger or bringing XRP to other ecosystems. And I think that's only going to continue. We want to see XRP available everywhere to everyone. And good to start with Solana as well and our favorite mid-tier manager, for sure.

Vibhu Norby: Thanks for coming Luke, it's awesome to have you here.

Luke Judges: Thanks man. Thank you for having us as well. Appreciate it.

sources.title: XRP needs a Solana-style strategy to keep up: Ripple executive
sources.snippet:
Ripple Exec Says XRP Needs a Solana-Style Playbook to Keep Up
[![Cointelegraph](https://cointelegraph.com/icons/logo/en.svg)](https://cointelegraph.com/)
Ecosystem
[](https://twitter.com/cointelegraph)[](https://telegram.me/cointelegraph)[](https://www.facebook.com/cointelegraph)[](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCRqBu-grVX1p97WaX4d-OuQ)
English
News
Indices

[Podcasts](https://cointelegraph.com/podcasts)
About
[
Liza Savenko](https://cointelegraph.com/authors/liza-savenko)
Dec 08, 2025
# XRP needs a Solana-style strategy to keep up: Ripple executive
Luke Judges highlights how XRP Ledger’s tech and developer tools could take cues from Solana to stay competitive among layer-1 networks.
Listen0:00
![XRP needs a Solana-style strategy to keep up: Ripple executive](https://images.cointelegraph.com/cdn-cgi/image/format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=90,width=1434/https://s3.cointelegraph.com/uploads/2025-12/019afeca-bd37-7393-8cd1-c962d2c8d834)
How to
COINTELEGRAPH IN YOUR SOCIAL FEED
## Key takeaways:
* Luke Judges states that technical strength alone cannot guarantee long-term competitiveness, suggesting that XRP could benefit from Solana’s pragmatism and execution speed.
* Judges believes Solana’s market traction comes from practical engineering and a fast go-to-market strategy rather than protocol design alone.
* David Schwartz takes the opposite position, arguing that XRPL’s reliability and stability are more valuable than pursuing high-throughput chains.
* Judges highlights that developer onboarding, tooling and validator incentives are critical for sustaining growth and reducing centralization risks.
Luke Judges, global partner success lead and director of Ripple, shared observations about the evolving XRP Ledger ecosystem and its competitive landscape, highlighting a clear parallel to the operational successes of rival[layer-1 network](https://cointelegraph.com/learn/articles/a-beginners-guide-to-understanding-the-layers-of-blockchain-technology)Solana. Drawing on his prior experience in the Solana network managing a substantial validator, Judges suggested that technical superiority alone is not enough to secure a network’s long-term relevance.
This article explores Ripple executives’ insights on operational lessons, focusing on technical advancements within the[XRP Ledger (XRPL)](https://cointelegraph.com/learn/articles/ripple-vs-xrp-vs-xrp-ledger)and the strategic requirements for layer-1 competitiveness.
## Operational lessons from Solana’s playbook
Judges’ perspective is unique and rooted in his experience operating two startups and running a Solana validator that managed more than $30 million in staked tokens through a full market cycle. He[shared](https://x.com/luke_judges/status/1994912330841559085)this detail on Nov. 30, 2025, on X, noting that he witnessed the network’s major price peak as well as its subsequent collapse and recovery.
This hands-on exposure led Judges to conclude that the success of layer-1 networks in a competitive cycle is often driven by factors distinct from core technology. He specifically credited Solana with having “pragmatism and speed,” which he views as essential for securing developer mindshare and driving adoption.
The core idea is that execution velocity and a practical approach to engineering and market entry can outweigh theoretical leadership in the race for ecosystem growth.
Nonetheless, Judges suggests that other chains could take note of how Solana runs its network, arguing there is “no point burying your head in the sand pretending you’re the only chain in town.” For the XRPL, these observations highlight potential blind spots, suggesting that technical milestones must be paired with a proactive go-to-market (GTM) strategy to translate into a true competitive edge.
![](https://s3.cointelegraph.com/uploads/2025-12/019afecb-94eb-7485-b39e-8da105c608fe)## Technical developments in the XRP Ledger
The call for strategic acceleration comes as the XRPL is actively pursuing

tags.impact_reason: XRP integration on Solana is a solid update, bringing a new asset to Solana DeFi, but the impact is limited by the need for wrapped assets and reliance on third-party bridges.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic data bridge as the Semantic Anchor because the core story is LayerZero creating a 'bridge' to move the XRP asset from its isolated ledger to the Solana ecosystem. The visual metaphor is direct and powerful. For the homepage, I integrated the text 'XRP' as a holographic projection emanating from the bridge's structure. This diegetically identifies the asset being transferred across this new connection, directly telling the story of 'XRP on Solana' without using subtitles.
visual_idea.description: A monumental, shimmering data bridge made of chrome and light connects two distinct floating landmasses across a dark digital canyon. One landmass glows with Solana's signature purple and green, the other with XRP's cool silver. Rivers of glowing data particles flow across the bridge from the silver side to the purple side. Integrated into the bridge's central archway, the letters 'XRP' are projected as a massive, stable, silver hologram, illuminating the data flowing through it. The background is a vast, deep digital void with faint grid lines suggesting infinite space.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A monumental, shimmering data bridge, crafted from gleaming chrome and ethereal light, spans an immense, dark digital canyon with polished obsidian-like surfaces. The bridge robustly connects two distinct floating landmasses: one pulsating with Solana's vibrant signature purple and green hues, the other radiating XRP's cool, reflective silver. Across the bridge's vast expanse, rivers of glowing, holographic data particles, appearing as luminescent, energetic torrents, flow dynamically from the silver XRP landmass towards the purple and green Solana landmass. Integrated majestically into the bridge's central archway, the letters 'XRP' are projected as a massive, razor-sharp, stable, three-dimensional silver hologram, casting an illuminating glow upon the data streams passing through it. The background is a vast, deep digital void, infused with dramatic volumetric light shafts and subtle atmospheric haze, with faint, intricate grid lines suggesting infinite, epic space. The composition is vertical (3:4 aspect ratio), emphasizing the immense height and epic scale of the bridge, with ample negative space above the structure to enhance its monumental presence. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, volumetric lighting, photorealistic, cinematic contrast.

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a futuristic data bridge as the Semantic Anchor because the core story is LayerZero creating a 'bridge' to move the XRP asset from its isolated ledger to the Solana ecosystem. The visual metaphor is direct and powerful. For the homepage, I integrated the text 'XRP' as a holographic projection emanating from the bridge's structure. This diegetically identifies the asset being transferred across this new connection, directly telling the story of 'XRP on Solana' without using subtitles.
visual_idea.description: A macro shot focusing on the point where the holographic data bridge connects with the Solana landmass. We see the intricate, interwoven strands of light and polished obsidian that form the bridge's structure. Concentrated streams of silver data particles, representing XRP liquidity, are seen flooding into the purple and green crystalline structures of the Solana ecosystem. The focus is on the texture of the light and the energy of the connection point.

final_prompt.prompt_text: A tightly framed, symmetrical macro shot, centrally focused on the precise connection point where an intricate holographic data bridge merges with a Solana landmass. The bridge's structure is composed of interwoven strands of dynamic, glowing light and dark, highly polished obsidian. Concentrated streams of brilliant silver data particles, representing XRP liquidity, are seen flooding energetically into the vibrant purple and lush green crystalline structures that form the Solana ecosystem. The entire frame is filled with extreme, intricate texture, highlighting the shimmering quality of the light and the reflective sheen of the obsidian, emphasizing the palpable energy of this digital conduit connection. Volumetric lighting casts dramatic, ethereal glows and sharp reflections across all surfaces, enhancing the ultra-realistic, hyper-detailed textures. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, photorealistic, cinematic contrast.

editorial.headline: Hex Trust and LayerZero Bridge XRP to Solana Ecosystem
editorial.standfirst: This strategic integration marks the end of the XRP Ledger’s decade-long isolationism, unlocking deep liquidity for Solana’s high-velocity DeFi markets. RippleX’s pivot to interoperability aims to position XRP as a cross-chain reserve asset rather than a siloed token.
key_takeaways: **Hex Trust** and **LayerZero** will issue wrapped XRP on Solana using the OFT standard, enabling direct DeFi integration.
The move grants XRP access to **Phantom**’s projected ~20 million active wallets and Solana’s prediction markets.
**RippleX** confirmed broader interoperability plans involving **Wormhole** and **Axelar** to ubiquitize the asset.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
The announcement that **Hex Trust** and **LayerZero** are bridging XRP to **Solana** represents a fundamental shift in **Ripple**’s institutional strategy. For over a decade, the **XRP Ledger** operated as a closed loop—an 'island' in Luke Judges' words. By sanctioning this bridge, **RippleX** is effectively conceding that utility must be exported to where the developers are. For **Solana**, this is a massive capital injection; XRP holders, often dormant in terms of DeFi participation, now have a direct pipeline to high-yield primitives like perps and prediction markets.

The 'Reserve Currency' thesis presented by Judges offers a glimpse into Ripple's endgame. Drawing a parallel to the USD, which requires global distribution to maintain hegemony, Judges argues XRP must exist ubiquitously across chains to survive. This is not just about bridging a token; it is about embedding XRP as a collateral asset within **Solana**'s deep liquidity pools. The choice of **LayerZero** as the interoperability standard suggests a focus on omnichain fungibility rather than bespoke, fragile bridges.

Looking ahead, this integration serves as a blueprint for Ripple’s broader interoperability roadmap, which includes **Wormhole** and **Axelar**. The immediate friction point will be user migration; however, access to **Phantom**’s ~20 million active wallets removes significant UX barriers. For institutional investors, the play is clear: monitor the flow of wrapped XRP into Solana’s lending markets, as this will likely compress yields while driving volume for the underlying bridge infrastructure.
